title: RsaRawOp enum exposes raw modular exponentiation honestly; padding is caller's responsibility
description: RsaRawOp names the four directions (PrivateEncrypt, PrivateDecrypt, PublicEncrypt, PublicDecrypt) that map 1:1 to wolfCrypt's RSA_* constants, and the doc comment and method name (raw_function) explicitly state no padding is applied. This is honest: wolfHSM's wh_Client_RsaFunction performs raw modular exponentiation. A future contributor might try to add PKCS1v15Sign/PSS-Sign variants here and implement padding in the wrapper. wolfHSM does not perform padding in the server — the primitive is genuinely raw. Wrapping it in a function that names it 'sign' without making padding explicit would mislead callers into thinking the protocol is handling PKCS#1 framing. The current design correctly forces the caller to understand what they're asking for.
